title: Limonium transcanalis
taxonomy: Limonium transcanalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. This plant is a member of the Plumbaginaceae family, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the plumbagin family. Limonium transcanalis is further classified as belonging to the genus Limonium, which includes a wide variety of coastal plants. The species is native to the Canary Islands. It is a part of the monocotyledonous flowering plants, as it belongs to the order Caryophyllales.

intro: Limonium transcanalis, also known as sea lavender, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small purple flowers and aromatic properties. The health benefits of sea lavender include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and wounds.
